feature: Provides a channel agnostic claim reporting solution for customers (broker and insured)
challenges:
-Brokers and Insureds want the choice of reporting channels (Mobile, Web, Email, Fax, Phone) and expect a carrier to provide them with all options 
-Carriers and customers desire FNOL set up (claim # assignment) and adjuster assignment as quickly as possible in order to ensure timely investigation, evaluation reserving and resolution.  Anything more than 4 hours is often perceived as too long leading to adverse impacts on the claim and the customer experience.   
-An FNOL contains a number of data points which are important to capture quickly and accurately so they can be immediately used to predict claim outcomes, properly reserve, report losses to underwriting, price risks and manage aggregate loss costs. 
-Significant effort and cost is needed to convert unstructured, semi-structured and structured documents, embedded files, notes, ACORD forms, scans, images etc...) to useable formats.
-Customers often complain of insufficient transparency into the claim assignment process.  They do not receive timely notification of the claim number nor the adjuster ID leading to unnecessary phone calls and emails and ultimately poor customer satisfaction.  .  
-Customers and regulators require data privacy and security for all PHI, PII information shared when reporting a loss.
gap_description:
Capability exists.  
1. Full scale multi-channel implementation has not been done with any client to date 
2. Mobile interface and reporting does not exist today

feature: Intelligent data extraction from bills/invoices sent via paper/email/fax to enable automated entry and into payment systems
challenges:
-Insurers make a significant number of claims payments annually. It’s an expensive process — not just in terms of the physical costs of printing, paper, and postage, but human beings are needed to answer questions, manage accounting, and oversee reconciliation. It’s a very non-strategic backroom process.
'-Today’s claimants expect fast, transparent claims payments with the ability to choose the payment mechanism — including newer mechanisms such as Venmo or Zelle. 
'-Vendor recipients want a method that makes it simple and efficient for them to process and reconcile as well. -Claims payment recipients expect much more flexibility in how their payment is handled and transparency in the process history. 
-Individuals expect to be able to choose how they want to receive their money — whether by check, debit card, ACH, Venmo, or other mechanisms. 
-Businesses don’t want to be overwhelmed with multiple payments on individual transactions; they want taxes and liens to be handled appropriately; and they also want self-service capabilities to track and reconcile payments.
-An increasing number of insurers are questioning the overall costs of handling payments. Rather than modifying their software systems in order to provide new capabilities, they’re deciding to outsource the entire process.
gap_description:
1. Robust and functioning extraction capability
2. A functioning bolt on interface that could work with various systems

solution:
Digitally Transforming Workers’ Compensation Claims 
Optimizing Medical and Disability Management
feature: AI Powered platform to manage medical case assignment, co-ordinate appointments (Physicians, Diagnostics etc.) and order services (Therapy, testing, Medical Supplies, transportation etc.)
challenges:
-The economic slowdown over the last several years has put pressure on both growth and profitability. There has been a significant shift in the split between medical costs and indemnity costs on a claim.  
-Variations and inconsistencies in state WC laws and reporting requirements increase cost and decrease efficiency.  
-Carriers need to create holistic treatment and medical management programs to deal effectively with the physical, emotional and psychological aspects of pain.
-Opportunity to reduce claim lifecycle and expedite claim payments - medical and indemnity
-Opportunity to manage return to work more effectively and reduce claim cost
-Opportunity to reduce medical payment fraud, waste and abuse
-Opportunity to reduce penalties as this continues to be a pain point for most WC insurers.
-Opportunity to improve communication between the injured worker, employer and carrier 
-Opportunity to improve/optimize cost management and disability management programs using data and analytics
-Reduce costs associated with UR and Medical Bill Review programs
-Streamline and digitize ingestion and intake of medical documents and bills

Potential Covid Impacts on WC
- There could be an increase in the # of compensable claims arising in frontline, Covid related situations.  However, this could be offset by a decrease in WC claims due to the increased # of employees WFH
- Carriers may see a decrease in the # of PT sessions that are deemed to be not immediately critical (likely only a short term effect)
- In economic downturns, workers may forgo filing claims for minor injuries to maintain active employment leading to a temporary downward pressure on claim frequency
-Will medical costs go up as Covid related injuries require extensive medical/hospital care?
-Will death claims see an increase?
-What happens to nurse case management?
- IME's may be delayed along with other types of medical care
- Will claims, therefore, stay open longer and thereby increase indemnity and medical costs
- Court systems have been closed - and will continue to be closed - creating backlogs and delays - will this also cause claims to be open longer and therefore drive up costs?
-Tighter investigations in Covid cases are required to prevent fraud and prove compensability
-Leverage telemedicine as a way to injured workers' to keep appointments while social distancing (not allowed in all states but there has been some easing of restrictions)
- So far, actuarial estimates about the potential cost of Covid have been all over the map
- 2 of the largest rating agencies aren't even guessing how much covid-19 might cost in permanent disability benefits - despite evidence that the virus causes long lasting ill health effects
- State governors and regulators are changing WC laws on the fly - it is continually evolving - making it difficult for a carrier to predict loss severity, reserve adequacy, rates etc.
gap_description: -Platform or bolt-on solution that could work with multiple client and medical provider systems for case management, appointment co-ordination and ordering services
